---
title: "Lizard's Tail (Saururus cernuus)"
slug: "saururus-cernuus"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622182919/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Lizard's Tail"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Piperales"
tax_family: "Saururaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Piperales", "Saururaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/622182919/medium.jpg" alt="Lizard's Tail" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/hoskissbirder">
hoskissbirder</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/341752703">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Piperales
-   **Family:** Saururaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=60742)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Rare species in Florida found in wetlands, swamps, marshes, and along stream edges.

## Natural history notes

A perennial wetland plant characterized by its heart-shaped leaves and distinctive arching spikes of small white flowers that resemble a lizard’s tail. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as swamps, marsh edges, and along streams. It typically blooms in late spring to summer and provides cover and habitat for wildlife in shaded wetlands.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/60742-Saururus-cernuus)
-   [Local profile](https://plant-directory.ifas.ufl.edu/plant-directory/saururus-cernuus/)
